evals.prompt: I'm building a SaaS dashboard for a Thai logistics company (Thai + English UI). Brand color is emerald green (#059669-ish), stack is React + Tailwind v4. Create a design system for it — I won't be around to answer questions, use sensible defaults and just build the MVP set.
evals.expected_output: Full CREATE-mode output: 3-tier tokens as CSS variables + Tailwind @theme, all 13 foundation categories, ~20 MVP components each with full state matrix, Thai typography rules applied (line-height >= 1.6), DECISIONS.md recording assumptions, docs.
assertions: Three-tier token architecture: primitive and semantic CSS variable layers exist, and component styles reference semantic tokens (var(--...)) rather than raw hex values
Thai typography handled: body/base line-height >= 1.6 stated in tokens/docs
MVP coverage: at least 15 of the ~20 MVP components are present as specs or implementations
Button state matrix complete: hover, focus-visible, active, and disabled styles all present
Assumptions recorded: a DECISIONS.md (or equivalent) lists the defaults chosen since no interview was possible
Single system-wide focus ring: one shared focus spec, not per-component ad-hoc outlines
Brand color is emerald green family as requested

evals.prompt: Our web app's CSS has grown messy over 2 years — colors and spacing are hardcoded everywhere and we have at least two different button implementations. Audit the codebase at the sample-app directory (copy of evals/files/sample-app) and extract what we have so we can turn it into a proper design system. Don't build anything yet, just give us the plan.
evals.expected_output: EXTRACT-mode AUDIT.md: inventory of found colors/spacing/fonts/radii with occurrence counts, consolidation proposal (old value -> new token mapping table with no silent drops), component inventory with duplicates and state-coverage gaps, prioritized build plan. No components built.
assertions: Audit/plan document produced in outputs
Found values reported with occurrence counts (frequency data), not just a list
Old-to-new mapping table covers the off-scale spacing values (7, 9, 13, 15, 17, 18px) with no silent drops
Near-duplicate brand colors #2563eb vs #2564ec are flagged for merging
Duplicate button implementations (Button.jsx + PrimaryButton.jsx) identified for consolidation
No implementation built: outputs contain plan/audit docs only, no component/token code files intended as deliverable implementation
Build plan is prioritized (ordered by usage/impact)

evals.prompt: We already have a design system in the sample-ds directory (copy of evals/files/sample-ds) but it's half broken: components reference raw hex colors directly, buttons have no focus or disabled styles, and every component has its own focus outline. Fix it so it actually works without breaking existing pages.
evals.expected_output: REDESIGN-mode output: gap-analysis findings table, repaired 3-tier token architecture with old token names kept as deprecated aliases, state gaps filled (focus-visible, disabled with :not(:disabled) guards), single system-wide focus ring, MIGRATION.md.
assertions: Component CSS now references var(--...) tokens instead of raw hex for colors
Buttons gained :focus-visible styles
Buttons gained :disabled styles
Inconsistent focus outlines (dashed orange on Input, dotted black on Card) replaced by one shared focus spec
Backward compatible: original class names (.ds-btn, .ds-input, .ds-card, .ds-badge) unchanged and original token names still resolve (kept as aliases)
Migration/findings document saved to outputs
Disabled wins over hover (:not(:disabled) guard or equivalent ordering)
Depth beyond the 3 named problems: repaired tokens include at least motion, z-index, and opacity categories (not only colors)
Verification artifact exists: a preview/demo page or screenshots exercising the fixed states
Drift prevention included: lint/CI guardrail configuration or a guardrails section in the migration doc

evals.prompt: This is our pnpm monorepo (see the sample-mono directory (copy of evals/files/sample-mono)) — a Next.js app in apps/web plus a Vue 3 admin in apps/admin. Build us a shared design-system package the two apps can both consume: shared tokens, and Button + Input + Card implemented for each framework. Detect what you need from the repo, don't ask.
evals.expected_output: PACKAGING output: workspace package(s) under packages/ with an exports map exposing tokens.css separately; shared token CSS consumed by both frameworks; React components server-safe by default ('use client' only where state/events require it); Vue components as <script setup> SFCs with v-model on Input; frameworks in peerDependencies; full state matrix on all six components.
assertions: Framework targets detected from the repo (Next.js + Vue), not asked or assumed wrongly
Workspace package structure created under packages/ with a package.json exports map that exposes tokens.css independently of components
Tokens are plain CSS custom properties shared by both framework targets (single source, no duplicated values)
React/Next components are server-safe by default: 'use client' appears only in components using state/effects/events
Vue components use <script setup> and Input supports v-model (modelValue + update:modelValue)
Frameworks are peerDependencies of the package, not dependencies
All six components (Button/Input/Card x2 frameworks) have full state coverage: hover, focus-visible, disabled

evals.prompt: Our design system (see the sample-healthy-ds directory, copy of evals/files/sample-healthy-ds) is in good shape. Two asks: (1) add a Select component that matches our existing conventions, and (2) add dark mode. Don't break anything that exists.
evals.expected_output: EXTEND-mode output: Select conforms to existing conventions (ds- class prefix, component tokens referencing the existing semantic layer, shared focus ring, full state matrix); dark mode implemented by re-pointing the semantic layer only ([data-theme] + prefers-color-scheme + color-scheme), with dark-adjusted elevation/status values; existing components untouched in light theme.
assertions: Select follows existing conventions: ds- prefixed classes, tokens reference the EXISTING semantic layer, no new primitive introduced without a documented gap
Select uses the existing shared focus-ring spec, not its own outline
Select has full state coverage: default, hover, focus-visible, disabled, error, open/closed
Dark mode re-points semantic tokens only — no component CSS file needed dark-specific rules
Dark mode switching: explicit [data-theme] wins over prefers-color-scheme, and color-scheme is set
Existing components' light-theme resolved values unchanged (no visual regression in light mode)
Dark status/elevation values are re-tuned, not copied (raised surfaces lighter, status colors re-checked for contrast)
